added: Stacked horizontal bar chart of total findings by severity for busybox:1.37, redis:7-alpine, alpine:3.20, ubuntu:24.04 and redis:7, appended to the Real Scan Results section with a caption calling out the same-app/different-base contrast and noting the counts are a separate 2026-07-04 run not comparable to the April table above.

Stacked horizontal bar chart (busybox:1.37, alpine:3.20, node:22-alpine, golang:1.23-alpine, golang:1.23, node:22) at the end of 'What you give up, and what you gain', captioned with the toolchain-vs-runtime gap and an explicit note that busybox is a near-empty-runtime proxy, not a distroless image.

Stacked horizontal bar chart (busybox:1.37, alpine:3.20, node:22-alpine, nginx:1.27-alpine, nginx:1.27, node:22) at the end of Step 1, captioned to show base-image choice is the largest single lever while stating alpine:3.20 still reports 301 so later steps still matter.
Stacked horizontal bar chart (busybox:1.37, alpine:3.20, golang:1.23-alpine, golang:1.23) at the end of Step 3, captioned that a multi-stage build fixes no CVEs but decides which row you ship.

Stacked horizontal bar chart (alpine:3.20, nginx:1.27-alpine, ubuntu:24.04, nginx:1.27) at the end of 'Why base-image patches lag behind', captioned that rebuilding narrows the gap but the base sets the floor.
